payload.name: Epic E-3 EKS Cluster
payload.description: Build a reusable Terraform EKS module that provisions an EKS v1.29 cluster, cluster IAM role, OIDC provider, managed node group with ARM64 t4g.small instances, node IAM role, access entry for the deploying principal, and core EKS add-ons. Then wire the module into the dev and prod environment root modules using VPC module outputs and verify with terraform fmt and terraform validate.

goal.answer: Build a reusable Terraform EKS module that provisions an EKS v1.29 cluster, cluster IAM role, OIDC provider, managed node group with ARM64 t4g.small instances, node IAM role, access entry for the deploying principal, and core EKS add-ons. Then wire the module into the dev and prod environment root modules using VPC module outputs and verify with terraform fmt and terraform validate.

payload.plain: Build a reusable Terraform EKS module that provisions an EKS v1.29 cluster, cluster IAM role, OIDC provider, managed node group with ARM64 t4g.small instances, node IAM role, access entry for the deploying principal, and core EKS add-ons. Then wire the module into the dev and prod environment root modules using VPC module outputs and verify with terraform fmt and terraform validate.

criteria.answer:
terraform/modules/eks/ contains versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, outputs.tf with provider constraint ~> 5.0 and required_version >= 1.6.0
EKS module creates an aws_eks_cluster named petclinic-{env} with Kubernetes version 1.29, public endpoint, and logging enabled for api, audit, authenticator
EKS module creates a cluster IAM role with AmazonEKSClusterPolicy attached
EKS module creates an OIDC provider from the cluster identity issuer
EKS module creates a managed node group with t4g.small instances (ARM64/Graviton), min_size=2, max_size=4, desired_size=2, disk_size=20, in public subnets
EKS module creates a node IAM role with AmazonEKSWorkerNodePolicy, AmazonEKS_CNI_Policy, and AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly attached
EKS module creates an access entry or aws-auth configuration granting cluster admin access to the deploying IAM principal
EKS module installs core EKS managed add-ons: coredns, kube-proxy, vpc-cni
EKS module outputs cluster_name, cluster_endpoint, cluster_ca_certificate, oidc_provider_arn, oidc_provider_url, node_group_name, and node_role_arn
terraform/environments/dev/main.tf calls the EKS module and passes vpc_id, public_subnet_ids, eks_cluster_sg_id, and eks_node_sg_id from module.vpc outputs
terraform/environments/prod/main.tf calls the EKS module and passes vpc_id, public_subnet_ids, eks_cluster_sg_id, and eks_node_sg_id from module.vpc outputs
terraform fmt -recursive -check terraform/ passes
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/dev
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/prod

plain: terraform/modules/eks/ contains versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, outputs.tf with provider constraint ~> 5.0 and required_version >= 1.6.0
EKS module creates an aws_eks_cluster named petclinic-{env} with Kubernetes version 1.29, public endpoint, and logging enabled for api, audit, authenticator
EKS module creates a cluster IAM role with AmazonEKSClusterPolicy attached
EKS module creates an OIDC provider from the cluster identity issuer
EKS module creates a managed node group with t4g.small instances (ARM64/Graviton), min_size=2, max_size=4, desired_size=2, disk_size=20, in public subnets
EKS module creates a node IAM role with AmazonEKSWorkerNodePolicy, AmazonEKS_CNI_Policy, and AmazonEC2ContainerRegistryReadOnly attached
EKS module creates an access entry or aws-auth configuration granting cluster admin access to the deploying IAM principal
EKS module installs core EKS managed add-ons: coredns, kube-proxy, vpc-cni
EKS module outputs cluster_name, cluster_endpoint, cluster_ca_certificate, oidc_provider_arn, oidc_provider_url, node_group_name, and node_role_arn
terraform/environments/dev/main.tf calls the EKS module and passes vpc_id, public_subnet_ids, eks_cluster_sg_id, and eks_node_sg_id from module.vpc outputs
terraform/environments/prod/main.tf calls the EKS module and passes vpc_id, public_subnet_ids, eks_cluster_sg_id, and eks_node_sg_id from module.vpc outputs
terraform fmt -recursive -check terraform/ passes
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/dev
terraform validate passes in terraform/environments/prod

constraints.answer: AWS region us-east-2 for both environments, All-public subnet design (no NAT Gateway, no private subnets), Node instances must be t4g.small ARM64 (Graviton) with AL2_ARM_64 AMI type, Cluster and node IAM roles must use least-privilege AWS managed policies only, Cluster and node groups must be placed in public subnets from the VPC module, Naming convention: petclinic-{env}-{resource}, Required tags: Project=petclinic, Environment={dev|prod}, ManagedBy=terraform, Do not modify the application repo, Do not run terraform apply or create real AWS resources

plain: AWS region us-east-2 for both environments
All-public subnet design (no NAT Gateway, no private subnets)
Node instances must be t4g.small ARM64 (Graviton) with AL2_ARM_64 AMI type
Cluster and node IAM roles must use least-privilege AWS managed policies only
Cluster and node groups must be placed in public subnets from the VPC module
Naming convention: petclinic-{env}-{resource}
Required tags: Project=petclinic, Environment={dev|prod}, ManagedBy=terraform
Do not modify the application repo
Do not run terraform apply or create real AWS resources

phases.answer: Implement EKS module, Wire EKS into environments

phaseSnapshots.name: Implement EKS module
phaseSnapshots.goal: Create the reusable EKS Terraform module with cluster, IAM roles, OIDC, managed node group, access entry, and add-ons

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/eks/versions.tf with Terraform and AWS provider constraints
Create terraform/modules/eks/variables.tf with required inputs (project, environment, vpc_id, subnet_ids, cluster_sg_id, node_sg_id, cluster_version, tags, node group sizing)

verification.command: grep -q 'variable "vpc_id"' terraform/modules/eks/variables.tf && grep -q 'variable "subnet_ids"' terraform/modules/eks/variables.tf && grep -q 'variable "node_instance_types"' terraform/modules/eks/variables.tf

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/eks/main.tf with cluster IAM role, EKS cluster, OIDC provider, node IAM role, managed node group, access entry, and core add-ons

verification.command: grep -q 'aws_eks_cluster' terraform/modules/eks/main.tf && grep -q 'aws_eks_node_group' terraform/modules/eks/main.tf && grep -q 'aws_iam_openid_connect_provider' terraform/modules/eks/main.tf && grep -q 'aws_eks_access_entry' terraform/modules/eks/main.tf

steps.description: Create terraform/modules/eks/outputs.tf with cluster_name, cluster_endpoint, cluster_ca_certificate, oidc_provider_arn, oidc_provider_url, node_group_name, node_role_arn

verification.command: grep -q 'cluster_name' terraform/modules/eks/outputs.tf && grep -q 'cluster_endpoint' terraform/modules/eks/outputs.tf && grep -q 'oidc_provider_arn' terraform/modules/eks/outputs.tf
cd terraform/modules/eks && terraform init -backend=false && terraform validate

phaseSnapshots.name: Wire EKS into environments
phaseSnapshots.goal: Call the EKS module from dev and prod root modules, pass VPC outputs, and validate both environments

steps.description: Update terraform/environments/dev/main.tf to call module "eks" and pass VPC outputs
Update terraform/environments/dev/variables.tf to declare EKS input variables
Update terraform/environments/dev/terraform.tfvars with dev EKS values (cluster_version=1.29, node_instance_types=["t4g.small"], min=2, max=4, desired=2)
Update terraform/environments/prod/main.tf to call module "eks" and pass VPC outputs
Update terraform/environments/prod/variables.tf to declare EKS input variables

assumptions.answer: VPC module (E-2) is complete and exports vpc_id, public_subnet_ids, eks_cluster_sg_id, eks_node_sg_id; Provider and backend configuration in terraform/environments/dev/ and prod/ already exists from Epic E-1; terraform init has already been run in both environment directories; AWS account and credentials are available for terraform init/validate

payload.summary: Created the reusable EKS Terraform module with versions.tf, variables.tf, main.tf, and outputs.tf. The module provisions an EKS v1.29 cluster with logging, cluster IAM role, OIDC provider, managed node group with t4g.small ARM64 nodes, node IAM role, access entry for the deploying principal, and core add-ons. Module passes terraform fmt and terraform validate.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.summary: Phase 2 completed: dev and prod root modules now call the EKS module, pass VPC outputs, declare EKS variables, provide environment-specific tfvars, and both environments pass terraform fmt and terraform validate.

grade.rationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

payload.finalSummary: Epic E-3 EKS Cluster is complete. The reusable Terraform EKS module was created under terraform/modules/eks/ with all required resources (EKS v1.29 cluster, IAM roles, OIDC provider, managed node group, access entry, core add-ons, and outputs). Both dev and prod root modules now call the EKS module, pass VPC outputs, declare EKS variables, provide environment-specific tfvars, and pass terraform fmt and terraform validate.

grade.rationale: The visible diff is dominated by unrelated .claude file region-string changes, documentation updates, and scripts; it does not substantiate the required Terraform artifacts (terraform/modules/eks/*.tf, terraform/environments/dev/main.tf, terraform/environments/prod/main.tf) that the success criteria and final summary depend on. Although the phase gates cite real terraform fmt and terraform validate commands, the key EKS module files are hidden in the truncated/untracked file list, so coverage remains suspect and unverified from the provided evidence.
